Name: Crowdstrike - Incidents Connector
Author: admin
Description: Deprecated. Pull incident and related behaviors from Crowdstrike. Dynamic List works with the “incident_type” parameter.

Parameters.Description: API root of the Crowdstrike instance.
Client ID  of the Crowdstrike account.
Client Secret of the Crowdstrike account.

Number of hours before the first connector iteration to retrieve incidents from. This parameter applies to the initial connector iteration after you enable the connector for the first time, or used as a fallback value in cases where connector's last run timestamp expires. Default: 1
Describes the name of the field where the environment name is stored. If the environment field isn't found, the environment is the default environment.

A regex pattern to run on the value found in the "Environment Field Name" field. Default is .* to catch all and return the value unchanged. Used to allow the user to manipulate the environment field via regex logic. If the regex pattern is null or empty, or the environment value is null, the final environment result is the default environment.

Lowest severity score of the incidents to fetch. If nothing is provided, the connector will ingest incidents with all severities. Maximum is 100. Note: action also supports the following values: Low, Medium, High, Critical. In the Crowdstrike UI the same value is presented as divided by 10.
How many incidents to process per one connector iteration. Default: 10. Max: 100.

Parameters.DisplayName: Use dynamic list as a blocklist
Parameters.Description: If enabled, the dynamic list will be used as a blocklist.
If enabled, connector will ignore the overflow mechanism.
If enabled, verify the SSL certificate for the connection to the Crowdstrike server is valid.
The address of the proxy server to use.
The proxy username to authenticate with.
The proxy password to authenticate with.
The customer ID of the tenant in which to execute the integration. For use in multi-tenant (MSSP) environments.
